name: Alicia's Market
tagline: 30+ poke varieties in a Kalihi market — the OG that every other poke shop tries to imitate.
mustOrder: Shoyu ahi poke — the classic that built this place's reputation
Spicy ahi poke — creamy and perfectly balanced heat
Hawaiian-style poke — simple limu and sea salt, old-school preparation
parking: Small lot in front of the store on Dillingham Blvd
talkStory: Founded by the Sniffen family and named after matriarch Alicia, this Kalihi market has been serving 30+ varieties of fresh poke since the 1970s — long before poke bowls became a mainland trend. The shoyu ahi and spicy ahi are legendary, but ask the counter staff what came in freshest that morning. Best selection before 11am; popular flavors sell out by afternoon. Cash only. Pair with a Bishop Museum visit five minutes away.
